title: Listening - Part 4: Festival Experiences
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about attending different types of festivals. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I went to this massive arts festival in Scotland. [21]I’d originally planned to go with my sister, but she bailed at the last minute, so I ended up going alone to prove I could do it[/21]. I was terrified of feeling like a loner. Actually, [26]it was the sheer quality of the street performances that blew me away[/26]. I’ve seen professional theatre, but the talent on those street corners was just world-class. It made me realise you don't need a fancy stage to be brilliant. || Speaker 2: My friends dragged me to a food festival. [22]I’m quite a picky eater, so I went mainly to try and expand my culinary horizons a bit[/22]. I’m tired of eating the same five meals. It was a bit overwhelming, but [27]I was really impressed by how eco-friendly the whole setup was[/27]. Everything was compostable, and there was zero plastic. It’s rare to see a large-scale event take sustainability so seriously, and I really respected that. || Speaker 3: I’ve always been a film buff, so a week at an international film festival was a dream. [23]I was actually there to scout for potential collaborators for a short film I’m writing[/23]. I needed to network. I did meet some great directors, but [28]the highlight was the Q&A sessions after the screenings[/28]. Hearing the creators talk about their struggles and process was so much more insightful than any textbook I’ve ever read. || Speaker 4: I attended a traditional folk music festival in the mountains. [24]I was doing some research for a university project on regional dialects[/24], so I spent most of my time recording conversations. It was fascinating from a linguistic point of view. What I didn't expect, though, was [29]how much I’d enjoy the sense of belonging to a community[/29]. Everyone was so welcoming, regardless of where you were from. It felt like I’d found a second home. || Speaker 5: I went to a tech and innovation summit. [25]The main draw for me was seeing a keynote speaker whose books have basically shaped my entire business philosophy[/25]. I just wanted to be in the same room as him. The talks were good, but [30]I actually got more value out of the informal workshops[/30]. We had to solve problems in real-time with strangers, and that hands-on approach really helped me consolidate what I’d learned in theory.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) each speaker's main reason for attending the festival.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 went alone 'to prove I could do it' after their sister cancelled.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted to 'expand my culinary horizons' because they are a picky eater.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 was there to 'scout for potential collaborators' and network.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 was doing 'research for a university project' on dialects.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to see a 'keynote speaker' who shaped their philosophy.

options.F: to hear a specific person speak
options.H: to escape a stressful work environment

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker found most impressive or beneficial.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 was impressed by the 'world-class' talent of the street performers.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 respected how 'eco-friendly the whole setup was'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 found the Q&A sessions 'insightful' and better than textbooks.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 enjoyed the 'sense of belonging to a community' and the welcome.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 got more value from 'informal workshops' and solving problems.

options.A: the variety of the food on offer
options.C: the practical nature of the activities
options.D: the high standard of the performances
options.E: the beauty of the surrounding area
options.G: the chance to win a competition
